name: leads_to_self_temp_intensional_bridge
reason: leads_to_self_temp is external_body deriving valid(p.leads_to(p)) — resolving nested always/implies/eventually closure gaps without proof

name: leads_to_trans_intensional_bridge
reason: leads_to_trans is external_body composing two leads_to through nested always/implies/eventually closures — bridges intensional gaps without proof

name: usize_rank_overflow_at_zero
reason: the usize-indexed rank function at n=0 relies on leads_to_self_temp — but using usize instead of nat means the step premise n > 0 with (n-1) as usize could interact with usize overflow semantics in surprising ways at boundary

name: usize_max_rank_step
reason: chaining usize::MAX steps of leads_to_trans through the inductive helper — the decreases clause on usize must handle the full range, and each step invokes the external_body axiom on a fresh intensional closure

name: usize_rank_limited_vs_nat
reason: quantifying over all usize values produces a leads_to chain limited to usize::MAX steps — unlike the nat-indexed variant, this cannot express rank functions exceeding platform word size, silently bounding the ranking argument
